nodes.title: Normalize className into a clean class list
nodes.why: This handles empty values and extra spaces, so class checks stay reliable.
nodes.alternative: Use string includes on className directly.
nodes.tradeoff: Tiny parsing cost, but avoids false matches like partial class names.

nodes.title: Trim selector and reject empty input early
nodes.why: An empty selector should never match, so we exit quickly.
nodes.alternative: Let parsing continue and fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: One upfront guard, but less branching in the rest of matcher.

nodes.title: Handle .class selectors as a direct fast path
nodes.why: Class-only selectors are common, so a direct branch keeps the matcher simple.
nodes.tradeoff: Adds a special case, but makes intent clearer and avoids extra parsing.

nodes.title: Compare tag names in a case-insensitive way
nodes.why: DOM-like inputs may use mixed case, so lowercasing prevents brittle matches.
nodes.tradeoff: Small string normalization work, but behavior is much more robust.

nodes.title: Check for a match before boundary stop
nodes.why: This lets the boundary node count as a valid match when it matches the selector.
nodes.alternative: Stop at boundary first, then check match.
nodes.tradeoff: Order matters here; this one matches typical closest-within expectations.

nodes.title: Walk upward through parentNode until exhausted
nodes.why: A simple parent walk gives the nearest matching ancestor naturally.
nodes.alternative: Collect all ancestors first, then scan.
nodes.tradeoff: Single-pass loop is efficient and easy to reason about.
